RESIDENT OF AMBASSADOR CLUB CONDOS IN THE 1900 BLOCK OF GULF SHORE BLVD NORTH OBSERVED A WATERSPOUT MOVING ONSHORE. PICTURES RECEIVED INDICATE MINOR DAMAGE TO ROOFING AND DOORS, ALONG WITH BROKEN TREE BRANCHES AND A SNAPPED SMALL SABLE PALM. ISOLATED NATURE AND MAGNITUDE OF DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH WATERSPOUT OBSERVATION. TORNADO RATED AN EF-0 WITH MAXIMUM WINDS LIKELY IN 65-80 MPH RANGE. POWER LINES DOWNED AND TREE DAMAGE IN APPROX. REPORTS RELAYED BY COLLIER COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT OF MOSTLY TREE DAMAGE IN EAST NAPLES FROM BARCELONA CIRCLE AND BEECHWOOD LAKE RD AREAS NORTHWEST TO INTERSECTION OF PENNY LANE AND PALM DRIVE. DISTANCE OF DAMAGE PATH ABOUT 1.7 MILES. SOME RELATIVELY MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE ALSO REPORTED. FURTHER INFORMATION TO BE PROVIDED ONCE STORM SURVEY IS COMPLETED LATER THIS EVENING. TIME OF REPORT BASED ON RADAR DATA.
THERE WAS A POSSIBLE TORNADO TOUCHDOWN ALONG TAMIAMI TRAIL AND RATTLESNAKE HAMMOCK ROAD IN EAST NAPLES. LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED A DAMAGE PATH ASSOCIATED WITH THIS POSSIBLE TORNADO.

*** 1 INJ *** PROBABLE BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN AT NORTH COLLIER HOSPITAL ON CORNER OF IMMOKALEE AND GOODLETTE-FRANK ROADS IN NORTH NAPLES AREA. DAMAGE REPORTED TO TREES AND LIGHT POLES, BUT NO STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. A PERSON WAS STRUCK BY A DOWNED TREE LIMB AND WAS TREATED ON-SITE. TORNADO LIKELY SPAWNED BY SAME STORM THAT PRODUCED EARLIER TORNADO IN EAST NAPLES.
THERE WAS A POSSIBLE TORNADO TOUCHDOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF PENNY LANE AND PALM DRIVE IN NAPLES. LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED ROOF DAMAGE ASSOCIATED WITH THIS POSSIBLE TORNADO.

*** 1 INJ *** A POSSIBLE TORNADO PASSED OVER NORTH COLLIER HOSPITAL IN NAPLES. DAMAGES CONSISTED OF DOWNED TREE LIMBS AND BROKEN STREET LIGHTS ON A PARKING GARAGE. ONE PERSON WAS STRUCK BY ONE OF THE DOWNED TREE LIMBS AND WAS TREATED.

AT 1134 AM EDT...GLADES COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE RELAYED A REPORT OF A POSSIBLE TORNADO NEAR PINE AVENUE IN MUSE. A MEMBER OF PUBLIC REPORTED THAT A BARN ROOF WAS TORN OFF.

AT 1150 AM EDT...A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED THAT A TORNADO FORMED JUST EAST OF GOODLAND AND WAS MOVING EASTWARD. THE TORNADO CROSSED US-41...TAMIAMI TRAIL...AND WAS HEADING TOWARD I-75...ALLIGATOR ALLEY. REPORT DELAYED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.

AT 118 PM EDT...A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 62 MPH. THIS GUST WAS ESTIMATED USING A HANDHELD ANEMOMETER. LOCATION OF REPORT WAS IN HOLLYWOOD BEACH NEAR HOLLYWOOD BOULEVARD AND A1A.

BROADCAST MEDIA RELAYED A REPORT OF POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGE IN WEST PALM BEACH AT 122 PM EDT. RAILROAD CROSSINGS WERE BLOWN DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BANYAN BOULEVARD AND TAMARIND AVENUE. TREE DAMAGE WAS ALSO REPORTED ALONG AUSTRALIAN AVENUE...CLEARWATER DRIVE AND TAMARIND AVENUE.

UPDATED AT 445 PM EDT. GOLDEN BEACH POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT THAT CAME ASHORE NEAR THE STRAND AT 400 OCEAN BOULEVARD AT 205 PM. HOUSES BEGAN TO BE AFFECTED IN THE 500-BLOCK OF OCEAN BOULEVARD AS THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHWARD. 625 OCEAN BOULEVARD WAS THE AREA MOST IMPACTED. A METAL GATE ENTRANCE TO A CONDO COMPLEX WAS TWISTED...AND A GARAGE DOOR AND 2 GATES WERE BLOWN OPEN. NO STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED. PRELIMINARY RATING IS A LOW-END EF0...STORM SURVEY EXPECTED MONDAY.

AT 300 PM EDT...GOLDEN BEACH POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT THAT CAME ASHORE NEAR THE STRAND IN THE 400-BLOCK OF OCEAN BOULEVARD AT 205 PM. DEBRIS FIELD WAS SPREAD ALONG A 6-BLOCK AREA. DAMAGE INCLUDED A GARAGE DOOR BEING BLOWN OPEN AND TREES DOWNED. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
